data.voice_note: Factual, measured, no hype, no medical claims, no unverifiable superlatives. Sentence case in body copy; standard release conventions.
data.headline: Rasa launches an AI meal planner that plans a month of delivery to your macros and budget, ordered one tap at a time
data.subhead: The India-first app plans a 30-day meal schedule around a user's dietary goals and a hard monthly budget, then places each order through Swiggy only after a one-tap confirmation — no silent auto-ordering.
body: Rasa, a new India-based food-tech app, today opened early access to a service that plans a full month of meals around a user's estimated macro targets and a set monthly budget, then orders each meal from delivery platforms after an explicit one-tap confirmation.

The app is built for people who already order food delivery frequently. After a short one-time preference interview — covering diet type, allergens, budget, goals and dislikes — Rasa generates a 30-day plan that aims to meet the user's calorie and macro targets while staying within a hard monthly spending cap. At each mealtime, the app re-checks availability and price, applies any coupons, and prompts the user to confirm before placing the order.

Rasa does not order or spend anything without the user's confirmation. Allergens flagged by the user are blocked before an item can be added to a cart, and a visible ledger tracks planned versus actual spending against the user's cap.

Nutrition information in the app is presented as an estimate. Rasa grounds dish estimates in an India-native food database and targets macros across the full day rather than claiming per-dish precision. The company states that the figures are wellness estimates and are not medical advice or diagnostic claims.

At launch, Rasa places orders through Swiggy. The company says support for additional food sources — including other delivery platforms and tiffin services — is planned, with the goal of managing a single plan and budget across multiple sources over time.

Rasa is offered as a subscription positioned around planning and budgeting rather than as a per-order fee, with a core tier priced from ₹149 to ₹299 per month and higher tiers planned for later. The company says it does not add a surcharge to the price of a user's food.
Early access is available now via the Rasa waitlist.

quotes.quote: Most people who order in every day aren't looking for another app to manage — they want one less decision and a bill they can predict. Rasa plans the month and does the legwork, but the person always taps to confirm. Smart, never silent.
We are careful to call nutrition figures what they are: estimates. The value is everyday balance and a budget that holds, not a medical promise.

data.boilerplate: About Rasa: Rasa is an India-based food-technology app that plans a month of meals around a user's estimated macro targets and monthly budget and helps them order those meals from delivery platforms, one tap at a time. Rasa is built on the principles of one-tap confirmation, allergen hard-blocking, a visible spend ledger, and clearly labelled nutrition estimates. The name 'Rasa' refers to the Sanskrit idea of taste and balance. Learn more at {{website}}.
data.disclaimers: Nutrition figures referenced are wellness estimates and not medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Market and pricing details are subject to change. Platform availability may vary by city.
